message:
feat(ft-ombfl.10): GPU harness frame-time reporter

tests/gpu_regression.rs:
  - Per-fixture render_ms / compare_ms / elapsed_ms accumulated into
    Vec<PerfEntry> as fixtures run. Captures the (optional) glyphs_
    cached / fonts_loaded / texture_format from the headless renderer.
  - End-of-run perf-summary JSON-line: phase=perf-summary with
    per_fixture, total_render_ms, p95_render_ms, fixture_count,
    runner, regressions_vs_baseline, aggregate_regressions.
  - perf-report.json written to target/gpu-regression/ (override via
    --perf-report=<path> or GPU_HARNESS_PERF_REPORT env var).
  - --perf-baseline=<path> compares current run to a baseline, flags
    per-fixture render_ms regressions >20% (configurable via
    --perf-threshold-per-fixture=N) and aggregate total_render_ms /
    p95_render_ms regressions >10% / >30% (--perf-threshold-aggregate=N).
  - --update-perf-baseline writes the current run as the baseline
    (gated by SET_PERF_BASELINE=1 sentinel for non-interactive runs).
  - --perf-self-test exercises three scenarios (real regression, pure
    improvement, sub-threshold wobble) plus pct_increase / compute_p95
    invariants.

Per the bead's risk note, perf regressions are warning-only in this
iteration: they appear in JSON-line output and perf-report.json but
do not block merge. Visual fixture failures still gate normally.

Bundles together with cod_2's just-shipped src/gpu_regression.rs
comparator extraction (ft-ombfl.11) — added `pub mod gpu_regression`
to lib.rs and fixed a `prop_assert!(matches!(... { .. }))` format-
string issue per MEMORY.md guidance (extract to bool, then assert).

Verified locally on macOS:
  --self-test       → pass
  --perf-self-test  → pass
  cargo check -p frankenterm-gui --tests --no-default-features → 0 errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
